homepage_url_justification: Project homepage is the pkgdown site https://bart-turczynski.github.io/rurl/ (also linked from DESCRIPTION URL).
sites_https_justification: All project sites (GitHub repo, github.io pkgdown site, CRAN, r-universe) are served over HTTPS.
description_good_justification: DESCRIPTION and README provide a clear, succinct description of what rurl does.
interact_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md explains how to interact with and contribute to the project.
contribution_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md is present at the repository top level.
contribution_requirements_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md states contribution requirements (tests, passing lint, style).
license_location_justification: LICENSE and LICENSE.md are at the repository top level.
floss_license_justification: Released under the MIT license (DESCRIPTION: 'MIT + file LICENSE').
floss_license_osi_justification: MIT is an OSI-approved open source license.
documentation_basics_justification: Basic documentation in README, the pkgdown reference site, and package vignettes.
documentation_interface_justification: Every exported function is documented via roxygen2 man pages (man/), published on the pkgdown reference site.
repo_interim_justification: Interim development commits are publicly available on GitHub between releases.
repo_distributed_justification: git is a distributed version control system.
version_unique_justification: Each release carries a unique version (DESCRIPTION Version field; CRAN enforces uniqueness).
version_tags_justification: Releases are tagged in git and published as GitHub Releases (e.g. v1.4.0).
release_notes_justification: NEWS.md documents user-facing changes for each release.
release_notes_vulns_justification: No publicly known vulnerabilities have been fixed to date; NEWS.md records all changes.
report_tracker_justification: GitHub Issues serve as the bug tracker.
report_process_justification: The issue tracker and CONTRIBUTING.md describe how to report bugs.
enhancement_responses_justification: Maintainer responds to enhancement requests on the tracker.
report_archive_justification: GitHub Issues are publicly archived and searchable.
vulnerability_report_process_justificati: Security policy published in SECURITY.md. Vulnerabilities are reported privately via GitHub private vulnerability reporting (repository Security tab -> 'Report a vulnerability'), with the maintainer email as a documented fallback; reporters are asked not to open public issues.
vulnerability_report_private_justificati: GitHub private vulnerability reporting (private security advisories) is enabled on the repository, providing a confidential channel visible only to the maintainers.
vulnerability_report_response_justificat: SECURITY.md commits to acknowledging reports within 7 days and to coordinating a fix and disclosure with the reporter.
build_justification: Builds with the standard R toolchain (R CMD build / INSTALL, devtools).
build_common_tools_justification: Uses the standard, widely available R build tooling.
build_floss_tools_justification: R and its toolchain are free/open source software.
test_justification: Automated test suite using testthat (edition 3) under tests/testthat/.
test_invocation_justification: Tests run via devtools::test() and as part of R CMD check.
test_most_justification: Test coverage is measured with covr and reported to Codecov; the suite covers most functionality.
test_policy_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md requires tests for new features and bug fixes.
tests_are_added_justification: New functionality is accompanied by tests, enforced in review.
tests_documented_added_justification: The test-addition policy is documented in CONTRIBUTING.md.
warnings_justification: Warnings are surfaced via R CMD check and lintr in CI.
warnings_fixed_justification: Warnings are treated as errors and fixed; R CMD check is kept clean.
warnings_strict_justification: CI sets LINTR_ERROR_ON_LINT=true so any lint fails the build; .lintr mirrors goodpractice's linter set.
know_secure_design_justification: The maintainer applies secure design principles; rurl defensively parses untrusted URL input.
know_common_errors_justification: The maintainer is aware of common URL/IDNA parsing pitfalls and tolerates malformed input safely.
crypto_password_storage_justification: rurl stores no passwords or credentials.
crypto_random_justification: rurl does not generate security-sensitive random values.
crypto_used_network_justification: rurl is a string/URL library and does not itself make network connections.
crypto_tls12_justification: rurl does not establish network connections.
crypto_certificate_verification_justific: rurl does not establish network connections.
crypto_verification_private_justificatio: rurl does not establish network connections.
delivery_mitm_justification: Distributed via CRAN and GitHub over HTTPS; CRAN publishes per-package checksums.
delivery_unsigned_justification: All distribution downloads are served over HTTPS (CRAN, GitHub, r-universe).
static_analysis_justification: lintr (full-package lint) and goodpractice are run; FOSSA scans dependencies for license/security issues.
static_analysis_common_vulnerabilities_j: R CMD check plus lintr cover common defect classes; FOSSA covers dependency vulnerabilities.
static_analysis_fixed_justification: Static-analysis findings are addressed before merge.
static_analysis_often_justification: lintr runs on every CI build (verify.yml), i.e. on each push and pull request.
dynamic_analysis_justification: Before releases the rhub workflow (.github/workflows/rhub.yaml) runs R CMD check -- including the testthat suite -- on the clang-ASAN/UBSAN and valgrind containers, exercising the code at runtime under memory and undefined-behavior instrumentation.
dynamic_analysis_unsafe_justification: rurl is a pure-R package with no compiled code of its own (no src/ directory), so it contains no memory-unsafe C/C++ code to fuzz; the ASAN/valgrind runs still cover its compiled dependencies.
dynamic_analysis_enable_assertions_justi: No project-authored compiled code, so there are no build-time or run-time assertions to enable during dynamic analysis.
dynamic_analysis_fixed_justification: Any issues surfaced by the sanitizer or valgrind runs are fixed before release.
test_continuous_integration_justificatio: GitHub Actions (verify.yml) runs lint + R CMD check + coverage on every push and pull request; full-check, rhub, and pkgdown workflows run as well.
discussion_justification: GitHub Issues (and Discussions) provide a public forum for project discussion.
no_leaked_credentials_justification: No credentials are committed to the repository.
english_justification: Documentation and issue-tracker interaction are conducted in English (Language: en-US).
hardening_justification: Hardening mechanisms (e.g. HTTP security headers) apply to web applications; rurl is an R library.
sites_password_security_justification: Project sites (GitHub, CRAN) manage their own authentication; rurl operates no site with user passwords.
maintained_justification: Actively maintained: recent CRAN releases, frequent commits, and responsive issue handling.
